reviews.review_msg: I found the lectures difficult to follow at times, and the exams were more challenging than I expected. Be prepared to spend extra time studying independently and reviewing the material outside of class.

This is a two credit course but I spent 20+ extra hours a week aside from attending every single lecture and all tutorials getting help from the Prof, TA, and my Tutor. My grade didn't reflect my efforts at all and it's tough. This is an impossible course with this prof unless you are a naturally capable chemistry student. Wait for someone else.

Took CHEM283. Study off of the textbook, you must learn by yourself. Tutorial attendance isn't mandatory, but you must go as problem set answers are given in tutorial. Exams are tough and time limited, practice as much as possible. Lecture notes are only surface level, the textbook is a must.

I do not believe she takes teaching seriously, her lecture scribbles are impossible to read and what is to be on the exam is not clear at all. If this class had any other professor I think the class would have done a lot better and learned a lot more. There are no lecture slides, she just does a worksheet on a projector. I regret taking this class.

option for chem 283 was not available (282 was the closest). tough grader. exams are HARD. on the second midterm the class did so bad she made the passing grade a 35%. even good students struggled! class is based off of exams ONLY. try to get a different prof if you need to take this class.

Most people didn't finish the final with a failing average too and that says a lot. I found it hard to follow her in lecture because the notes were also way simpler than what appeared on exams. You have to spend lots of time practicing with notes that aren't that great. I would not recommend.

Entire course graded by exams, so most students didn't do much work. You have to practice every problem she gives you and as many from the textbook as you can. Tests are extremely difficult, do not expect to finish, but do them right and you will be above the average which will be ~45%. You won't understand the material unless you go to lectures.

Makes you do the problem set then mark 1 question or parts of a question. Told me she 'does not have the time or space to deal with homework marks' after approaching her during office hours. Also makes you write a 9 page exam within 50 minutes expecting nobody to finish. The average was a fail for that exam.

Worst prof I've had at SFU in the 4 years I've attended by a landslide. She is a terrible test maker, every test is a rush with minimal time to think or even complete questions. Is a very harsh grader. I would never recommend this professor to anybody who enjoys chemistry. Averages on tests were extremely low, when the content is not hard.
Did research projects with her please don't ever join her lab. A living nightmare.

The lectures were good and her way of teaching course was better than the professor teaching the same course. However, She uses much of critical analysis stuff to be examined on exams. Her exams are hard and lengthy but if you are preparing from textbook and her notes you can do well.

I like her notetaking approach - it's clear and concise. Use it as a guide in reading the book - because it's how it's supposed to be for a difficult course. She actually did us a favor in cutting down a lot of topics from the chapters  - but other students were just not aware coz they haven't opened the book. Do problem sets and practice problems

tests were absolutely on a different level than what was gone over in class. if you want to even pass you have to read the textbook and/or do extra problems. what she teaches will just be the absolute baseline of what she expects you to know. and she goes through lecture material very quickly.

Dr. Plettner isn't as bad as expected tbh. Her lectures are clear and thought-out (can get tiring, though) and she wants students to do well. If you ask for help, she will definitely make time for you. Go to office hours if you can. 
The course is very exam-heavy, the final was worth 55% and the midterm was worth 45%.

Exams are quite hard and the averages don't look good, although generally speaking she is a fair and okay professor. If she scales the class properly (which we deserve) then I would even give her 3.5. However, comparing her with 281's Robert something, she is definitely a lot better as she would post the class notes on canvas.
